findings.title: Implement One-Click Purchase Flow
findings.description: Develop a one-click purchase flow for returning customers that utilizes saved payment methods to streamline the checkout process.
assumptions: Users have saved payment methods.
Users are familiar with the one-click purchase concept.
recommendations: Implement the one-click purchase feature as a priority.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a returning customer with saved payment methods WHEN they initiate checkout THEN they should see an option for one-click purchase that skips the review step.

findings.title: Address Checkout API Latency
findings.description: Optimize the checkout API to ensure that the p95 latency is reduced to meet the 500ms SLO, which is critical for maintaining user trust and enabling new features.
assumptions: Current latency issues are due to inefficient processing.
Optimizations can be implemented without major disruptions.
recommendations: Implement caching and reduce round-trips to the payment processor.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the checkout API WHEN latency is measured THEN p95 latency must be <= 500ms.

findings.title: Fix Payment Retry Reliability
findings.description: Resolve issues with payment retries that are failing silently due to idempotency key collisions and race conditions, ensuring users are not double-charged.
assumptions: The current retry mechanism is flawed.
Fixing this will restore user trust.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a payment retry scenario WHEN a payment fails THEN the retry must succeed without silent failures.

findings.title: Implement Auto-Apply Best Coupon Feature
findings.description: Automatically detect and apply the best available coupon at checkout to reduce friction and improve conversion rates.
assumptions: Users prefer not to manually enter coupon codes.
The system can accurately detect applicable coupons.
recommendations: Develop and test the auto-apply feature.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user at checkout WHEN they have applicable coupons THEN the best coupon must be automatically applied.

findings.title: Implement Social Proof Badges
findings.description: Display real-time purchase activity badges during checkout to enhance user confidence and potentially improve conversion rates.
assumptions: Users respond positively to social proof.
The feature can be implemented without significant performance impact.
recommendations: Conduct A/B testing to measure impact.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user at checkout WHEN they view the checkout page THEN they should see social proof badges indicating recent purchases.

findings.title: Improve User Experience Feedback Mechanism
findings.description: Establish a mechanism to gather user experience feedback during the checkout process to identify pain points and areas for improvement.
assumptions: Users are willing to provide feedback.
Feedback will be actionable.
recommendations: Create a feedback form or survey post-checkout.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user completes a checkout WHEN they receive a feedback prompt THEN they should be able to provide feedback on their experience.

findings.title: Monitor Payment Failure Rate
findings.description: Implement monitoring for payment failure rates to ensure that improvements to the checkout process do not negatively impact payment success.
assumptions: Monitoring tools can accurately track payment failures.
Alerts can be set up for high failure rates.
recommendations: Set up alerts for payment failure rates exceeding acceptable thresholds.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the payment processing system WHEN payment failures are tracked THEN alerts must trigger if failure rate exceeds 3%.

findings.title: Conduct A/B Testing for One-Click Purchase Flow
findings.description: Run A/B tests to evaluate the impact of the one-click purchase flow on checkout conversion rates.
assumptions: A/B testing can be effectively implemented.
Results will provide actionable insights.
recommendations: Analyze results and iterate on the one-click purchase feature.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the one-click purchase flow is implemented WHEN A/B testing is conducted THEN conversion rates must be compared against a control group.

findings.title: Enhance Checkout Page Stability on Mobile
findings.description: Fix issues causing the checkout page to crash on mobile devices, particularly iOS Safari and Android Chrome.
assumptions: The crashes are due to specific interactions with payment methods.
Fixing these will improve user experience.
recommendations: Conduct thorough testing on mobile devices post-fix.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the checkout page on mobile WHEN they switch payment methods THEN the page must not crash.

findings.title: Resolve Payment Processing Errors
findings.description: Investigate and fix issues causing payment processing errors where users are charged but the transaction shows as failed.
assumptions: The errors are due to webhook timeouts.
Fixing this will restore user trust.
recommendations: Implement a more reliable webhook handling mechanism.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a payment transaction WHEN a charge is confirmed THEN the transaction status must accurately reflect the payment outcome.

summary: The analysis of customer insights and metrics has led to the identification of critical requirements for the QuickPay checkout redesign. Key areas of focus include implementing a one-click purchase flow, addressing reliability issues, and enhancing user experience through feedback mechanisms and performance improvements. Each requirement has been structured with clear acceptance criteria and dependencies to ensure a comprehensive approach to improving the checkout process.
